item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that is used in this article was produced by the author and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh God, this freaks me the hell out. Just completely hits all my Horror Buttons. Take your upvote and get out of here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks - nice to wake up to this feedback. Very much appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
absolutely LOVE this, the escalation, the characterization — all very good. Maybe change the object class to "Safe Uncontained" with a footnote leading to the bit where Lazarus stole it - although that may compromise the reveal… hmmm……..
edit: hehehe 100th upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the comments, always appreciated.

Back story/Front story:

There's a load of tales starting with this one (and they're not over yet) starting from before the phone's containment to the weeks afterwards:

At The Library

And there's the squiky tale wherein a Class D gets turned into a mollusc to see what happens - Golan

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't this technically a reality bender? I kinda get the feeling that this was a person that was type green or stronger, and they messed up with something or in a fight. I could even imagine that the phone was trying to escape from something and disguised itself as this object to hide, but now it's stuck with only the phone's abilities.

I like it. I kind of want more backstory. Or front story, like Lazarus using and losing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the upvote, always appreciated!

It's Euclid rather than Keter because you can lock it in a box and it won't kill you, unless the phone has changed hands - in which case there would be some unknown process taking place whilst it's there… Basically if you just box it up it won't cause any new harm, but it'll still be working whatever grusesomeness was in progress before it was boxed up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoting for the sudden 'Oh my God it kills all of the previous users contacts'.

That was very unexpected for me and it lent this a nice bit of horror.

I do have to say though, given the abilities the phone has, wouldn't this be considered a Keter? Phone just has to swap hands a few times and that possibly hundreds of people dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can please all of the people all of the time, and you can please some of the people some of the time, but you can't please some of the people all of the time. Or all of the people some of the time, or some of the time all of the time.

Note to self: congrats on toning down villainy and removing reference to Lazarus twirling moustache whilst tying heroine to railway track in black and white silent film stylee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You couldn't have written a more obvious villain with Lazarus. If the name didn't tick me off at first, it's his total apathy towards humanity and the fact that he is to be suddenly given total access to the skip should. Yeah, he may be totally loyal at first, but you can see where this is going a mile away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's whatever you want it to be (hand-waves weakly and walks off stage to slow clapping).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! Anyway it wasn't dumb - how were you to know I would become the site's most best-loved, well-known and universally respected contributor?*

  • Findings as per non-independent survey of 1 site member conducted in May 2016.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Past me, why do you always say dumb things?

Wils, have an upvote one year after the fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Calling it: Televono is a subsidiary of The Factory.

Because no one produces anything in the MacCanon without some involvement from one of the Big Names.

This is seriously exactly how you'd think one of their mobile products to work, especially if it was high-end. Think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice. Some day I'd like to shoehorn Father Ted references into something…

What I've been doing lately, though, is featuring popular songs in my articles (I've done it for the only two articles I've published, and plan to do so for at least some of the articles I'm working on now). :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember now I was going to name the old couple in the photo in SCP-2962 George and Mildred, but i thought it would be too hammy :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the record I'm planning something similar with my own articles, so I guess that makes us both stupid. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a funny thing, but I thought so. I thought adding references to TV programmes was my signature, but looking at it in more detail I've only ever done it once, here.

I am slowly coming to realise how stupid I am, and I must say it's a comfort.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Forgot to ask, but is this line:

As always, I'll keep you in the loop re the current state of play.

a deliberate reference to british television? (State of Play is a miniseries, while the film In the Loop is the continuation of the series The Thick of it). Just wondering.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this, the effects are terrifying, and there's some really nice twists/fridge horror. You also set up an interesting villain.

Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cooties are a currently uncontained CK-Class restructuring event danger!

If you have any information on this SCP, please report to the nearest Site Director or their personal assistant, whichever matches your own gender identification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wooow. I just got major tingles. Upvote for sure. This went in an entirely different direction than I thought it was going and made me pleasantly ecstatic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaarrrgghh!!! It nearly got to 30!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder how I missed this being posted back in April.

That said, it doesn't strike my fancy, partly because of the tone, partly because it's just an OTT, super-anomalous cell phone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the thoughts (and the upvote) - I have a few ideas with this, maybe a scip or two about affected D_Class and employees. I'm also working on a couple of tales, one about the previous owner of the phone and one about the hapless Class D who got turned into a limpet as part of one of the tests (that was almost a throw-away line in the scip but I think it has lots of horror potential)… ALthough maybe that could be a clinical, stand alone test log? Dunno.

As regards the progression - I have to avoid painting myself into a corner. I know it's a very minor scip in the grand scheme of things but I still want it to be the best it can be, so I shall have to fink and fink again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ideas for going forward:

  • Tale centering on Lazarus (and his motivations, whether he was altered, etc.)
  • Tale centered on long-term effects of testing (wonder who all the D-class killed off when they went? Any major criminal organizations suddenly having power vacuums?)
  • Tale centered on O5-? (horror factor of sword-of-damocles)
  • Tale centered on operational issues caused by modified/deleted staff
  • "standard" SCP with operational issues from this overlapping (risky for public opinion)

There's SO much room to go on. Have you already determined Lazarus' actual plans? That's the first step. Where the phone goes/who dies/who changes is next. Finally, who wins? If you're going for a serial format, this can take multiple tales to achieve.

Hope this helps at all!

+1 for massively underestimated danger

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OK - class changed as that seems to be the majority opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that it should prolly be euclid. Even if it was safely contained, it still has some pretty reality-blasting powers, which have taken the foundation by surprise at least a couple times. I get what you were going for, and it's definitely not keter, but I don't think it's hurt by an euclid classification (euclid is not really THAT dangerous a classification, nowadays; a lot of them are just really weird).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, it looks more like this:

[[include component:image-block name=ImageName.jpg|caption=This text appears under the image.|width=300px]]

I'll fix it myself, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Zyn's code is the correct version. I posted a version without the width.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very much agree - however I'm trying to add "Televono Telefex Secrecy-8 Startup Screen" as a caption but I can't. Any clues?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I might change the class, I dunno. I agree totally with what you're saying re the reveal, fellow newbie, but sometimes you just gotta say "flick da rulz" :P haha wish I

I've got a lot of backstory because the article started off a lot longer, so I have interview logs etc etc that it would be fun (for me, at any rate, don't know about you lot) to somehow make use of.

Forwardstory (nice term) is something I'll need to work on, but at the moment I'm a) working on my second skip and b) working on my third, fourth, fifth etc etc skips. Will probably never get any of 'em done. Not having a clue how to do the forwardstory doesn't help, either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
doh I remember why it was SAFE now. This skip is all about the reveal. I used the safe category because when you start reading the narrative, it's contained. It's only until near the very end that the phone is revealed to be gone. I didn't want anything to make people think "why is it Euclid if its safely contained?" and I wanted to throw in some naughty misdirection.

Your SCP is still classified as 'safe'…. ????
Not sure about this 'cuz I'm a newbie, but I don't think you can do that. This is not supposed to be a tale, or short story; it's supposed to be a scientific report for use by members of an organisation. So there should be no 'reveal', or 'naughty misdirection' (though I like the expression! ;>). Minimum Euclid, and I would vote for Keter.

Had a look at your sandbox, will see what I can do (as soon as I figure out how…) but what I would like to see is not backstory, but forwardstory, if you see what I mean! What you say above about ways to neutralize the threat is interesting… More, please!
(what if all high-placed executives in the Foundation changed their names and/or cellphone numbers? rrh rrh rrh)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was kinda expecting the standard image formatting, at least with a caption where the logo came from. Considering it's not from a company that actually exists, I think it would make sense to have a small caption explaining where the Foundation actually got the image from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hi. What formatting would you expect to see? Genuine question. I just wanted the Televono logo, nice and simple. No text underneath it, no borders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there supposed to be image formatting, or is the lack of it deliberate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nobody likes cooties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reads like a mediocre creepy pasta mixed with a bad soap opera.

Also, nobody wants to wake up a different gender.

Because these highly trained, professional members of a paramilitary organization are terrified of… what, exactly? Cooties?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thankyou!

My first skip so I appreciate your comments. I'm currently working on some back story tales for some of the characters in my sandbox, under the phone tale tab. Hopefully they'll do ok (any feedback gratefully received, gang).

A serial? Hmmm… thinks…. I can think of two ways to neutralise the threat - one guaranteed to work but taking a loooooooong time, the other being a much bigger but immediate gamble that might or might not work (and it'll be difficult to know until it goes wrong).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WOW.
It seems to me that this is one of the few, if not the only, SCP that poses a direct, immediate and fatal threat to the very existence of the Foundation.
I mean, given the position of Lazarus in the Foundation, all he has to do is give the phone to someone else. Like 'Here, can you hold this for me please?'… and most, if not all, of the highest-placed members of the Foundation will die very shortly.
Right now, he's having fun with it in a more elaborate way (05-7 must be an attractive woman, I suppose… ;>), but… what if he tires of the whole thing and wants to go out with a bang? Especially if, indeed, someone has turned him from 'loyal friend' (and, even more disturbingly, 'loyal high-placed executive') into 'traitor'…
My comment is, therefore, 'GOOD JOB, vilifier, but… do you know where you're going with this? Is there a way to somehow neutralize the effects of the phone and to secure/contain/protect it?
(which is interesting, because this could be the first skip that's actually a series… Eagerly awaiting for next episode here!)
Okay. This said, I'm off to upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah with the "safe" thing I was coming from the angle of…. OK I got nuffin.

Changed to Euclid. (It was safe up until it got pinched, I guess)

EDIT: doh I remember why it was SAFE now. This skip is all about the reveal. I used the safe category because when you start reading the narrative, it's contained. It's only until near the very end that the phone is revealed to be gone. I didn't want anything to make people think "why is it Euclid if its safely contained?" and I wanted to throw in some naughty misdirection.

TBH I didn't think anyone would pick up on the right-class-but-wrong-class-really aspect of it.

I had SO many issues and rewrites with this b*stard that quite frankly I never want to change it again. I knew it was a good story but getting it to fit a skip, whilst not losing the narrative reveal, was an ordeal. But fun.

There. That makes me sound clevererer than my original reply.

Finally, many many thanks for your feedback, to get such praise makes the blood, sweat and tears worthwhile :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really one of the best new SCPs I've read in the past few months.
Congratulations author!
It starts out innocent enough but gains pace soon and becomes more threatening all the time.
I find it interesting that the phone is still classified as safe - sure it's easy to lock away, but at it's current state it's a viable threat to the whole Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I've cut the interview completely and amalgamated the exposition into Runeberg's final email, and made other trims too here and there. Let me know if that's better.

Aaaagh I know - don't do a new post etc etc - apols!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback. This IS the trimmed version :) it started out as a phone that ALSO had a carcinogenic, toxin-laden, deadly bacteria-generating touchscreen. That's how Harold Maine got caught originally - he left a trail of syphilis, e-coli, Ebola and tuberculosis wherever he went. That went out the window when I realised it was too much anomaly for not enough gain.

Put it down to inexperience but I can't trim much more without losing dramatic/narrative force. That said, this is still a work in progress (I know main site shouldn't be, but I've had some genuinely valuable feedback since posting which I've tried to incorporate).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. Upon a closer read, I've decided to change my downvote to a novote. It's a good idea, though I do feel like this could use some trimming for length… It gets a little draggy at parts, but that might be my personal perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem, Zyn (in response to the post below)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for that - they were conscious choices. It's a bit of a DIYD,DIYD situation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed the 05 name. I know you're under no obligation to do so as this is "mainsite", but if you can give any other examples of ignored canon I'd be grateful for this and future works.

"Ehh. This isn't incompetently written."

That's the effect I was going for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly? I thought this was great. Very nice escalation, plenty of twists to keep me engaged.

Some of the stylistic choices aren't entirely in line with what's on the rest of the site, but frankly, those kind of things keep me on my toes after seeing so many SCPs with text that you think you can skip over because it looks just like all of the other text on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback - it's already been on the draft site, and based on feedback from Eskobar I've made structural changes. What I've presented here today is the best way I can work this whilst keeping a) the narrative and b) the anomalies.

I think I'll play this by ear, as he was very receptive to the story I'm trying to unfold ((thankyou Eskobar for your feedback BTW)).

If it turns out that there are downvotes jumping on my ass I'll delete and try again - the most obvious thing to change being to remove the "subconscious texts" element and just leave the "contact info changes."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough - there is a reason for the way I did it, however if that way turns people off, I'll change it (i.e. make that important part an addendum and that discovery apart of the description (now done)).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehh. This isn't incompetently written. Over-detailed and redundant, maybe, and with some stuff that ignores consensus canon (O5s ate usually referred to by their number, for example), but not incompetently written. I'm gonna second Observer September here. Depending on its performance, I'd delete and start again. However, I'm personally no-voting, this time around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some people will downvote based in the rest of the wrong format. I'd make that important part an addendum and that discovery apart of the description- but do as you wish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for your feedback folks - Ah that bloody rating module. Doh! I wrote the SCP procedures in bullet point as I figured if you wanted to know NOW what you needed to do, you'd want it clear and easy to read.

However, you're right in that it's off-format, so I've changed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree; the idea is well executed and interesting- but the format if off.
Upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Format issues: you forgot the rating module, and containment procedures are written in paragraph form, not bullet form.

I was prepared to downvote this, but to be honest, the core idea of "phone that can change contact info in real life and/or send subconscious messages" is very interesting me, interesting enough that I'll refrain from doing so. I strongly encourage you to take this down and rewrite it with the help of IRC and the draft forum. This can be a very good SCP, it just needs a lil' more lovin'.

A suggestion: perhaps eliminate either the subconscious message or the contact info changes; focusing on one to the exclusion of the other might give you a more specific anomalous property to work with, and thus make the hook more interesting.
